office_does: One of two House seats for the 36th District (Ballard, Magnolia, Queen Anne, Greenwood area of northwest Seattle), serving two-year terms writing state law and budgets.
race_blurb: Incumbent Liz Berry, House Labor Committee chair and prime sponsor of the 2025 permit-to-purchase firearm law, is the only candidate filed and advances unopposed on the primary ballot.

candidates.summary: Third-term incumbent State Representative serving since 2021 and chair of the House Labor Committee, with a signature record on gun-safety legislation and a career in reproductive-rights and consumer advocacy.
highlights: Prime sponsor of HB 1163 (2025), Washington's permit-to-purchase firearm law, signed by Gov. Ferguson in May 2025 [S3]
Chairs the House Labor Committee; cites legislation protecting workers from wage theft and unfair noncompete agreements [S1]
Former Legislative Director for Congresswoman Gabby Giffords and past Pro-Choice Washington board member; has worked to protect and expand abortion access [S1]

taxes.basis: Supported tax cuts on essentials while 'making our tax system fairer' to fund services, and calls the 2026 tax-code rebalancing package 'a historic step.'

experience.basis: Third-term incumbent with committee chairmanship and a concrete legislative signature (HB 1163), though tenure is moderate.

social.basis: Lifelong reproductive-justice advocate (past Pro-Choice Washington board) with legislative work to protect and expand abortion access, plus signature gun-safety law.
